  • Internals and Porting Notes
    • Available internals pages
  • diCal2 Native Parity Notes
    • Why these changes were necessary
    • What changed
      • 1. Shared upstream/native result normalization
      • 2. VCF reader now preserves upstream-style physical-locus metadata
      • 3. Grouped-locus counting now uses physical blocks, not just segregating columns
      • 4. Refined intervals now materialize interval-specific epochs
      • 5. Meta-start generation was tightened toward upstream semantics
      • 6. Gallery rerun used a targeted diCal2-only render path
      • 7. Meta-start replay tracing is now available on the native path
      • 8. Java nextLong() and coordinatewise shuffle semantics were still off
      • 9. VCF-backed native configs needed to be compacted after filtering
      • 10. The remaining IM mismatch is concentrated in cross-pop PCL cases
    • What improved
      • 11. Structured no-growth native runs needed ODECore, not EigenCore
    • What still does not match
    • Next steps
  • PSMC Internals

    • Core model

    • What stayed out of the main docs
  • SMC++ Parity Closure Notes
    • Scope
    • Final state
    • What was actually wrong
      • 1. Native one-pop preprocessing was not phase-aligned with upstream
      • 2. Native one-pop HMM was leaving the upstream compressed-row ceremony
    • How the issue was found
    • The fixes that closed parity
      • Upstream preprocessing offset in native one-pop preprocessing
      • Stay on the compressed run-length stream in the one-pop HMM
    • Earlier fixes that mattered
      • One-pop Watterson estimator
      • Monomorphic one-pop emission handling
      • One-pop transition details
      • Prefit / initialization parity
      • Scale-step optimizer semantics
    • What did not close parity by itself
    • Practical lesson
    • Code pointers

smckit
  • API Reference
  • Tools: Inference Algorithms
  • View page source

Tools: Inference Algorithms

The inference tools. Each function takes an SmcData container, runs an SMC method, and writes results into data.results[<name>]. See Choosing a Method for help picking the right one.

All public smckit.tl algorithms follow the same implementation-provenance contract:

  • implementation="native" runs the in-repo implementation

  • implementation="upstream" requests the original tool when a bridge exists

  • implementation="auto" resolves to the best available path and currently prefers upstream when upstream is exposed

PSMC

smckit.tl.psmc(data, pattern='4+5*3+4', n_iterations=30, max_t=15.0, tr_ratio=4.0, alpha=0.1, mu=1.25e-08, generation_time=25.0, random_init=0.01, seed=None, implementation='auto', upstream_options=None, native_options=None)[source]

Run PSMC demographic inference.

Parameters:

  • data (SmcData) – Input data from smckit.io.read_psmcfa().

  • pattern (str) – Parameter pattern (default "4+5*3+4").

  • n_iterations (int) – Number of EM iterations.

  • max_t (float) – Maximum coalescent time (units of 2N₀).

  • tr_ratio (float) – Initial θ/ρ ratio.

  • alpha (float) – Time interval spacing parameter.

  • mu (float) – Per-base per-generation mutation rate.

  • generation_time (float) – Generation time in years.

  • random_init (float) – Amplitude of random initialization noise for λ.

  • seed (int, optional) – Random seed.

  • implementation ({"auto", "native", "upstream"}) – Algorithm provenance selector. "native" runs the in-repo port. "upstream" requests the original implementation and currently raises because no public upstream bridge is exposed yet. "auto" resolves to the best available implementation.

  • upstream_options (dict | None)

  • native_options (dict | None)

Returns:

Input data with results stored in data.results["psmc"].

Return type:

SmcData

eSMC2

smckit.tl.esmc2(data, n_states=20, rho_over_theta=1.0, n_iterations=20, estimate_beta=False, estimate_sigma=False, estimate_rho=True, beta=1.0, sigma=0.0, mu_b=1.0, mu=1.25e-08, generation_time=1.0, pop_vect=None, box_b=(0.05, 1.0), box_s=(0.0, 0.99), box_p=(2.0, 2.0), box_r=(0.0, 3.0), rp=(0.0, 0.0), rho_penalty=0.0, implementation='auto', backend=None, upstream_options=None, native_options=None)[source]

Run eSMC2 demographic inference.

Parameters are the same as the native implementation. implementation may be "native", "upstream", or "auto". "auto" currently prefers upstream when an executable R environment is available. backend is a deprecated compatibility alias.

Parameters:

  • data (SmcData)

  • n_states (int)

  • rho_over_theta (float)

  • n_iterations (int)

  • estimate_beta (bool)

  • estimate_sigma (bool)

  • estimate_rho (bool)

  • beta (float)

  • sigma (float)

  • mu_b (float)

  • mu (float)

  • generation_time (float)

  • pop_vect (ndarray | list[int] | None)

  • box_b (tuple[float, float])

  • box_s (tuple[float, float])

  • box_p (tuple[float, float])

  • box_r (tuple[float, float])

  • rp (tuple[float, float])

  • rho_penalty (float)

  • implementation (str)

  • backend (str | None)

  • upstream_options (dict | None)

  • native_options (dict | None)

Return type:

SmcData

MSMC2

Warning

MSMC2 is in development and not yet fully validated. See MSMC2.

smckit.tl.msmc2(data, n_iterations=20, time_pattern='1*2+25*1+1*2+1*3', mu=1.25e-08, rho_over_mu=0.25, fixed_rho=False, generation_time=25.0, stride_width=1000, max_distance=1000, quantile_bounds=False, time_factor=1.0, implementation='auto', upstream_options=None, native_options=None)[source]

Run MSMC2 demographic inference.

Parameters:

  • data (SmcData) – Input data from smckit.io.read_multihetsep().

  • n_iterations (int) – Number of EM iterations (default 20).

  • time_pattern (str) – Time segment pattern (default "1*2+25*1+1*2+1*3").

  • mu (float) – Per-base per-generation mutation rate.

  • rho_over_mu (float) – Initial ratio of recombination over mutation rate (default 0.25).

  • fixed_rho (bool) – If True, keep recombination rate fixed during optimization.

  • generation_time (float) – Generation time in years (for scaling output).

  • stride_width (int) – HMM stride width for expectation collection (default 1000).

  • max_distance (int) – Maximum distance for propagator precomputation (default 1000).

  • quantile_bounds (bool) – Use quantile boundaries instead of Li & Durbin (default False).

  • time_factor (float) – Multiplicative factor for time boundary computation (default 1.0).

  • implementation ({"auto", "native", "upstream"}) – Algorithm provenance selector. "native" runs the in-repo MSMC2 port. "upstream" currently raises because no public upstream bridge is exposed yet. "auto" resolves to the best available implementation.

  • upstream_options (dict | None)

  • native_options (dict | None)

Returns:

Input data with results stored in data.results["msmc2"].

Return type:

SmcData

MSMC-IM

smckit.tl.msmc_im(data, *, pattern='1*2+25*1+1*2+1*3', mu=1.25e-08, N1_init=15000.0, N2_init=15000.0, m_init=0.0001, beta=(1e-08, 1e-06), xtol=0.0001, ftol=0.01, implementation='auto', upstream_options=None, native_options=None)[source]

Fit an Isolation-Migration model to MSMC2 cross-population rates.

Takes MSMC2 combined output (within-pop and cross-pop coalescence rates) and fits a two-population IM model with time-dependent N1(t), N2(t), and symmetric migration rate m(t).

Parameters:

  • data (SmcData, str, or Path) – Either a SmcData object (results will be stored in data.results["msmc_im"]), or a path to a combined MSMC2 output file which will be read automatically.

  • pattern (str) – Time-segment pattern matching the MSMC2 run. Default "1*2+25*1+1*2+1*3" (MSMC2 default for 32 time segments).

  • mu (float) – Per-base per-generation mutation rate. Default 1.25e-8 (human).

  • N1_init (float) – Initial guess for population 1 effective size.

  • N2_init (float) – Initial guess for population 2 effective size.

  • m_init (float) – Initial guess for symmetric migration rate.

  • beta ((float, float)) – Regularization strengths (beta1, beta2). beta1 penalizes total migration; beta2 penalizes population size asymmetry. Default (1e-8, 1e-6).

  • xtol (float) – Powell optimizer parameter tolerance. Default 1e-4.

  • ftol (float) – Powell optimizer function tolerance. Default 1e-2.

  • implementation ({"auto", "native", "upstream"}) – Algorithm provenance selector. "native" runs the in-repo fitter. "upstream" runs the vendored MSMC_IM.py oracle when it is runtime-ready. "auto" resolves to the best available implementation and prefers upstream when the bridge is ready.

  • upstream_options (dict | None)

  • native_options (dict | None)

Returns:

Input data with results stored in data.results["msmc_im"].

Return type:

SmcData

ASMC

smckit.tl.asmc(data, *, pairs=None, mode='array', fold_data=True, skip_csfs_distance=0.0, scaling_skip=1, store_per_pair_posterior_mean=True, store_per_pair_map=False, implementation='auto', upstream_options=None, native_options=None)[source]

Run ASMC pairwise coalescence time inference.

Parameters:

  • data (SmcData) – Input data from smckit.io.read_asmc().

  • pairs (list of (int, int), optional) – Haplotype index pairs to decode. If None, decodes all unique pairs.

  • mode ({"array", "sequence"}) – “array” uses compressed/ascertained emissions; “sequence” uses classic emissions.

  • fold_data (bool) – Use folded CSFS tables.

  • skip_csfs_distance (float) – Minimum distance between CSFS sites (Morgans).

  • scaling_skip (int) – Apply scaling every this many positions.

  • store_per_pair_posterior_mean (bool) – Store per-pair posterior mean TMRCA.

  • store_per_pair_map (bool) – Store per-pair MAP state indices, matching upstream ASMC perPairMAP.

  • implementation ({"auto", "native", "upstream"}) – Algorithm provenance selector. "native" runs the in-repo decoder. "upstream" currently raises because the public upstream bridge is not exposed yet. "auto" resolves to the best available implementation.

  • upstream_options (dict | None)

  • native_options (dict | None)

Returns:

Input data with results stored in data.results["asmc"].

Return type:

SmcData

SMC++

smckit.tl.smcpp(data, n_intervals=32, max_t=15.0, alpha=0.1, mu=1.25e-08, recombination_rate=1e-08, generation_time=25.0, regularization=1.0, max_iterations=100, seed=None, implementation='auto', backend=None, upstream_options=None, native_options=None)[source]

Run SMC++ demographic inference.

Parameters are the same as the native implementation. implementation may be "native", "upstream", or "auto". "auto" currently prefers upstream when the controlled side environment is available. backend is a deprecated compatibility alias.

Parameters:

  • data (SmcData)

  • n_intervals (int)

  • max_t (float)

  • alpha (float)

  • mu (float)

  • recombination_rate (float)

  • generation_time (float)

  • regularization (float)

  • max_iterations (int)

  • seed (int | None)

  • implementation (str)

  • backend (str | None)

  • upstream_options (dict | None)

  • native_options (dict | None)

Return type:

SmcData

diCal2

smckit.tl.dical2(data, n_intervals=11, max_t=4.0, alpha=0.1, n_em_iterations=10, composite_mode='pac', loci_per_hmm_step=1, mu=1.25e-08, generation_time=25.0, n_ref=None, start_point=None, meta_start_file=None, meta_num_iterations=1, meta_keep_best=1, meta_num_points=None, bounds=None, seed=None, implementation='auto', upstream_options=None, native_options=None)[source]

Run diCal2 demographic inference.

Parameters:

  • data (SmcData) – Data container from smckit.io.read_dical2. Must have data.sequences of shape (n_haplotypes, seq_length) and data.uns populated with mutation_matrix, demo, config, n_alleles.

  • n_intervals (int) – Number of refined coalescent time intervals.

  • max_t (float) – Maximum coalescent time (units of 2N₀).

  • alpha (float) – Resolution near t=0.

  • n_em_iterations (int) – Number of EM iterations.

  • composite_mode ({'pcl', 'lol', 'pac'}) – Composite-likelihood scheme.

  • loci_per_hmm_step (int) – Number of loci to group into one HMM step, matching diCal2’s --lociPerHmmStep option approximately.

  • mu (float) – Per-base mutation rate (for converting to absolute time).

  • generation_time (float) – Years per generation.

  • n_ref (float, optional) – Reference effective population size for scaling. If None, derived from theta and mu.

  • start_point (array-like, optional) – Initial values for placeholder parameters in diCal2 ?N order.

  • meta_start_file (str, optional) – Tab-separated file of candidate starting points in diCal2 ?N order.

  • meta_num_iterations (int) – Number of meta-start generations to run when meta_start_file is provided. 1 means evaluate only the supplied points.

  • meta_keep_best (int) – Number of best points to retain between meta-start generations.

  • meta_num_points (int, optional) – Number of points per meta-start generation. Defaults to the number of rows in meta_start_file.

  • bounds (str or list of pairs, optional) – Parameter bounds in diCal2 placeholder order, e.g. '0.1,1;0.2,2'.

  • seed (int, optional) – RNG seed (currently unused — kept for API symmetry).

  • implementation ({"auto", "native", "upstream"}) – Algorithm provenance selector. "native" runs the in-repo diCal2 port. "upstream" runs the vendored diCal2.jar through the public upstream bridge when the Java runtime is ready. "auto" resolves to the best available implementation.

  • upstream_options (dict | None)

  • native_options (dict | None)

Returns:

Same container with results stored in data.results['dical2'].

Return type:

SmcData

Previous Next

Built with Sphinx using a theme provided by Read the Docs.
